name: FIBROUS HISTIOCYTOMA
definition: MFH = UNDIFFERENTIATED PLEOMORPHIC SARCOMA MALIGNANT FIBROUS XANTHOMA XANTHOSARCOMA = MALIGNANT HISTIOCYTOMA FIBROSARCOMA VARIANT

demographics.Histo: interlacing bundles of fibrous tissue in storiform pattern (whorled / woven)
demographics.Mean age: 50 (range, 10-90) years; peak prevalence in 5th decade; more frequent in
demographics.Size: usually 5-10 cm → increasing over months / years
demographics.Prevalence: 5% of all primary malignant bone tumors

dominant_finding: central hypodense area = myxoid MFH (DDx: hemorrhage, necrosis, leiomyosarcoma
discriminating_features: well-defined radiolucent lesion with septa / soap-bubble appearance / no definable matrix

general: well-defined radiolucent lesion with septa / soap-bubble appearance / no definable matrix
poorly defined curvilinear / punctate peripheral calcifications / ossifications (in 5-20%)
commonly cortical erosion of adjacent bone (HIGHLY SUGGESTIVE FEATURE)
CT: well-defined large lobulated soft-tissue mass with attenuation similar to that of
central hypodense area = myxoid MFH (DDx: hemorrhage, necrosis, leiomyosarcoma
mass of muscle density with hypodense areas (necrosis)
invasion of abdominal musculature, but not IVC / renal veins (DDx to renal cell
MR: low to intermediate SI similar to muscle on T1WI
heterogeneously intermediate to high SI on T2WI
contrast enhancement more pronounced in periphery than at center ← central location
myxoid tumor component similar to fluid
nodular + peripheral enhancement of nonmyxomatous cellular region
extensive mineralization / small areas of focal metaplastic calcification
expansion in smaller bones (ribs, sternum, fibula, clavicle)
occasionally lamellated periosteal reaction (especially in presence of pathologic
US: well-defined mass with hyperechoic + hypoechoic (necrotic) areas
